evidence.scientific_clarity: Stacked bars across 5 conditions with percent y-axis fully conveys the 4-class proportion brief; clean spines and gridless layout aid readability.
evidence.typography: fonttype=42 never set; PDF uses Type 3 bitmapped glyphs and SVG uses path-traced DejaVu glyphs — no selectable text anywhere; default DejaVu Sans font not replaced.
evidence.palette: Four distinct colors used; however green (#55A868) and red (#C44E52) appear together, failing deuteranopia/protanopia colorblind safety; blue and tan are fine.
evidence.layout_density: 6x4 in figure with tight_layout, bar_width=0.55, y-range 0-1 with 20% ticks — space is well used with no obvious whitespace or label overlap.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content and colors; legend is inside the plot area which may overlap bars depending on data; no numerical finding cited in caption.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF font is Type 3 (bitmapped, non-selectable); script sets no pdf.fonttype=42 or rcParams override — text is not editable vector in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (12K), figure.png (24K), caption.tex (344B), gen_figure.py (1.5K) all present and non-trivial; data.json referenced by script is absent from directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption correctly identifies chart type, classes, and color mapping, but contains no specific numerical finding or quantitative claim about the data shown.

overall_strengths: Clean, minimal layout with removed top/right spines, tight_layout, and percent-formatted y-axis fully satisfies the brief's stacked-bar requirement.
All required output files (PDF, PNG, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) are present and non-trivial in size.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is not set, producing Type 3 bitmapped glyphs in the PDF and path-traced glyphs in the SVG — text is non-selectable and non-editable, a critical vector fidelity failure.
Green/red color pair (#55A868 and #C44E52) is not safe for red-green colorblind readers, and the caption lacks any specific numerical finding to anchor the scientific message.
